Text: AI agents are more than prompts. This repo shows the runtime layer.

Harness Engineering Guide is a practical guide to building AI agent harnesses with concepts, tutorials, papers, tools, and runnable code examples.

It helps you understand what turns a bare language model into an agent by breaking down the harness pieces: tool execution, memory, context assembly, safety boundaries, scheduling, and multi-agent orchestration.

Key features:

• First-principles path – starts with “What is a Harness?” and a 50-line Python harness you can copy and run
• Core runtime concepts – covers the agentic loop, tool systems, memory, context, and guardrails
• Practice-focused chapters – includes sandboxing, skills, sub-agents, error handling, scheduling, and long-running harness design
• Reference comparisons – compares OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex, Cline, Aider, and Cursor side by side
• Community contribution flow – accepts resources through GitHub issues or direct PRs

It’s open-source (MIT license).

Link in the reply 👇
